subject: earth_space_sciences
question: How does the data in the graph support an explanation of the Big Bang theory?
choices:
Galaxies farther from Earth have greater recessional velocities, indicating an
expanding universe.

Galaxies closer to Earth have negative recessional velocities, indicating that the rate
of the expansion of the universe only occurs around distant galaxies.

Galaxies located approximately one million (106) parsecs away from Earth are
traveling toward and away from Earth, indicating that the universe does not expand
further than this distance.
Galaxies travel away from
answer:
Galaxies farther from Earth have greater recessional velocities, indicating an
expanding universe.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Which statement correctly explains the difference in the elements composing the two stars and
compares the lifespans of these two stars?
choices:
In Betelgeuse, helium has fused into heavier elements, and Betelgeuse has a shorter
lifespan than the Sun.

In Betelgeuse, hydrogen has yet to be fused from heavier elements, and Betelgeuse
has a longer lifespan than the Sun.

In the Sun, heavier elements have completely fused into helium, and the Sun has a
shorter lifespan than Betelgeuse.
In the
answer:
In Betelgeuse, helium has fused into heavier elements, and Betelgeuse has a shorter
lifespan than the Sun.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Other than ocean currents, what other factor determines the global circulation of these plastic
toys?
choices: stratosphere convection
prevailing winds
elevation
cloud cover
answer: prevailing winds
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Which statement explains how the removal of plastic bags in the GPGP will stabilize biodiversity
in the area?
choices:
Decreasing the number of bags in the GPGP will cause the jellyfi sh population to
increase because the turtles will eat more plastic bags.

Decreasing the number of bags in the GPGP will cause the turtle population to
increase because the turtles will eat more jellyfi sh.

Decreasing the number of bags in the GPGP will cause the jellyfi sh population to
decrease because the turtles will eat less jellyfi sh.

Decreasing the number of bags in the GPGP will cause the turtle population to
decrease because the turtles will eat less jellyfi sh.
answer:
Decreasing the number of bags in the GPGP will cause the turtle population to
increase because the turtles will eat more jellyfi sh.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
A student makes a claim that the river cleanup method is a more effective design solution than
the ocean cleanup method for reducing the impact of plastic pollution. Using all the information
provided, which statement provides the most correct evidence to support this claim?
choices:
The river method costs less than the ocean method and captures plastic before it
enters the ocean, protecting river and ocean coastline biodiversity.

The river method can collect plastic over a larger area and has lower maintenance
costs than the ocean method.

The river method is able to capture plastics of all sizes that are both fl oating and
underwater, while the ocean method only collects large plastic pieces.

The river method captures more plastic than the ocean method because more plastics
are found in the ocean than in the rivers.
answer:
The river method costs less than the ocean method and captures plastic before it
enters the ocean, protecting river and ocean coastline biodiversity.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question: Which statement identifi es the evidence used to determine when the Huronian ice age occurred?
choices:
Radioactive dating of elements in rocks was used to determine ages of rocks in the
Huronian Supergroup.

Index fossils were identifi ed in the Huronian Supergroup to determine the ages of
rocks.
Rocks in the Huronian Supergroup were carbon dated to determine their age.

The ages of the dropstones in the sedimentary rock are younger than the particles
that make up the rock itself.
answer:
Radioactive dating of elements in rocks was used to determine ages of rocks in the
Huronian Supergroup.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Which statement correctly uses information from the model and passages as evidence to support
the claim that, during the end of the Huronian ice age, changes in Earth’s hydrosphere caused
a change in Earth’s geosphere?
choices: Microfossils found in the banded iron formations produced the iron in the oceans.

Iron in the ocean was used for energy by the photosynthesizing stromatolites in the
shallow part of the ocean.

The presence of carbon dioxide and methane in the ocean led to the formation of
black smokers on the bedrock of the ocean bottom.

The presence of iron and oxygen in the oceans led to the formation of banded iron
rock layers on the bottom of the ocean.
answer:
The presence of iron and oxygen in the oceans led to the formation of banded iron
rock layers on the bottom of the ocean.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Based on the student’s observations, which set of observations and mineral name correctly
identifi es the mineral crystals in the photograph?
choices: Observations A, B, and C identify magnetite.
Observations D, E, and F identify olivine.
Observations A, C, and E identify garnet.
Observations B, D, and F identify hematite.
answer: Observations A, C, and E identify garnet.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Which statement below correctly identifi es a benefi t to a local community when comparing
these two design solutions for resurfacing roads?
choices:
Local quarries supplying natural paving materials will use more fuel for trucking and
processing.

Recycled asphalt pavement will use more petroleum products in the form of fuel and
emulsifi er.

Recycled asphalt pavement does not require reclamation and will extend the life span
of existing quarries.
Blasting, crushing, and processing costs are higher with recycled asphalt pavement.
answer:
Recycled asphalt pavement does not require reclamation and will extend the life span
of existing quarries.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

Which statement would provide evidence for the formation of Pluto’s four smallest moons from

choices: Pluto’s surface is coated with methane ice.
Craters with diameters as large as 240 kilometers exist on Charon’s surface.

Both

When the Storegga Slide triggered a tsunami, the initial wave would have caused a A

B . The location of C experienced less impact from the tsunami with a wave
height of about D .

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Based on the map, which evidence-based claim correctly identifi es how a change in climate
most likely affected the current pattern of ice thickness on Greenland?
choices:
Cooling of climate in polar regions caused no ice and more exposed land near
Baffi n Bay than along the Denmark Strait.

A warmer climate at lower elevations caused ice to be thickest near the center of
Greenland where it is over 3000 m and thins to 0 m along coastlines.

Cold climates caused ice ranges from 0 m at the edges to over 2500 m near the
GISP 2 drill location.

A warming climate produced no general ice-thickness pattern since land is exposed
along the edges of the entire coastline.
answer:
A warmer climate at lower elevations caused ice to be thickest near the center of
Greenland where it is over 3000 m and thins to 0 m along coastlines.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

Ice mass rate of change was a loss of about 250 Gt in 12 years.

The rate of change in ice mass loss was less from 2010 to 2022 than it was in any

From 2010 to 2022, ice mass loss was 3000
answer: Ice mass rate of change was a loss of about 250 Gt/year.

Bennu formed near the orbit of

Its speed increased due to Dimorphos’ gravitational pull.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Which statement identifi es how the construction of the Mount Morris Dam has impacted the
ability to control rain events?
choices:
Construction of the dam has controlled river elevations to increase the availability of
fresh water for the residents of Rochester.

Construction of the dam has controlled water levels in the Genesee River, reducing
the chance of fl ooding and property loss for residents of Rochester.

Construction of the dam has controlled river elevations so that more swimming areas
could be created within Letchworth State Park.
Construction of the dam has controlled the amount of erosion in the
answer:
Construction of the dam has controlled water levels in the Genesee River, reducing
the chance of fl ooding and property loss for residents of Rochester.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
When comparing gas-powered cars to electric cars, which statement correctly summarizes the
type of car that has a lower environmental impact related to the mining industry while providing
reliable transportation for long distances?
choices:
The electric car has a lower environmental impact from mining because the motor
only weighs 76 kilograms and has an average driving range of 234 miles.

The electric car has a lower environmental impact because it emits only 32 grams
per mile and uses only 53.2 kilograms of copper.

The gas-powered car has a lower environmental impact because it only uses 22.3
kilograms of copper to manufacture and has a median driving range of 403 miles.

The gas-powered car has a lower environmental impact because it costs about $500
more to operate than an electric car and releases only 411 grams of CO per mile.
answer:
The gas-powered car has a lower environmental impact because it only uses 22.3
kilograms of copper to manufacture and has a median driving range of 403 miles.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Which explanation uses evidence from the infographic to most correctly explain how human
activity is being infl uenced by the availability of copper used in the manufacture of new electric
vehicles?
choices:
As copper reserves are depleted, companies will be forced to lower the price of
copper below the 2019 average of $6042 per ton in countries with copper mines.

The fi ve countries with 65% of copper reserves will need to reduce copper
production to conserve resources in order to make more electric cars.

Since global copper production more than doubled between 1995 and 2019, all
countries with copper reserves will need to open new mines to meet the immediate
demand for the manufacture of electric cars.

Copper recycling will need to increase in order to meet the demand for electric car
manufacture beyond 105 years from now.
answer:
Copper recycling will need to increase in order to meet the demand for electric car
manufacture beyond 105 years from now.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Which evidence-based statement correctly describes how the electrical energy generation
resources in the United States are projected to change as the demand for electricity increases?
choices: By 2030, hydro will surpass nuclear electric generation.

By 2050, renewables will surpass natural gas as the largest source of electric
generation.
Energy generation from coal is projected to increase by 2035.
From 2020 to 2050, wind energy is projected to increase more than solar energy.
answer:
By 2050, renewables will surpass natural gas as the largest source of electric
generation.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question:
Which claim accurately identifi es a change in atmospheric oxygen levels due to a change in
Earth’s biosphere in the Carboniferous Period?
choices: Oxygen levels increased as a result of deforestation in the biosphere.
Oxygen levels increased as a result of decreased respiration in the biosphere.
Oxygen levels increased as a result of increased photosynthesis in the biosphere.
Oxygen levels increased as a result of decreased transpiration in the biosphere.
answer: Oxygen levels increased as a result of increased photosynthesis in the biosphere.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.

subject: earth_space_sciences
question: Which pieces of evidence from the maps and graph support the students’ claim?
choices:
An increase in glaciation added ice to the cryosphere and lowered ocean water levels
in the hydrosphere.

An increase in water levels in the hydrosphere were a result of melting ice from the
cryosphere.

Water levels in Earth’s hydrosphere and the amount of ice in the cryosphere both
increased.
Water levels in the hydrosphere and ice levels in the cryosphere both decreased.
answer:
An increase in glaciation added ice to the cryosphere and lowered ocean water levels
in the hydrosphere.
explanation: This is from the January 2026 Earth & Space Sciences Regents Exam.
